heading: Gateway communities
label: 8441 Gateway communities
notes: References in TextSection 5(b)(1)(B), referred to in subsec. (a)(1)(D)(ii), probably means . Section 5(b)(1)(B) probably referred to section 5(b)(1)(B) of H.R. 3200 introduced in the House in the 118th Congress on . A later version of H.R. 3200 was enacted as subtitle C of title I of , and, as enacted, does not contain a section 5. children.heading: Assessment of impacts and needs in gateway communities
children.label: a Assessment of impacts and needs in gateway communities
C
children.text: accommodation and management of sustainable visitation; and

children.label: i
children.text: existing developed locations that are underutilized on nearby Federal recreational lands and waters that are suitable for developing, expanding, or enhancing recreation use, as identified by the Secretaries; or

children.label: ii
children.text: existing developed and suitable lesser-known recreation sites, as identified under section 5(b)(1)(B), on nearby land managed by a State agency or a local agency; and1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: A
children.text: providing financial or technical assistance to a gateway community under an existing program;

children.label: B
children.text: entering into an agreement, right-of-way, or easement, in accordance with applicable laws; or

children.label: C
children.text: issuing an entity referred to in paragraph (1) a special use permit (other than a special recreation permit (as defined in ), in accordance with applicable laws.section 6801 of this title

children.heading: Technical and financial assistance to businesses
children.label: b Technical and financial assistance to businesses

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary of Agriculture (acting through the Administrator of the Rural Business-Cooperative Service), in coordination with the Secretary and the Secretary of Commerce, shall provide to businesses in gateway communities the assistance described in paragraph (2) to establish, operate, or expand infrastructure to accommodate and manage sustainable visitation, including hotels, campgrounds, and restaurants.

children.heading: Assistance
children.label: 2 Assistance
children.text: The Secretary of Agriculture may provide assistance under paragraph (1) through the use of existing, or the establishment of new, entrepreneur and vocational training programs, technical assistance programs, low-interest business loan programs, and loan guarantee programs.

children.heading: Partnerships
children.label: c Partnerships
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Secretaries may, in accordance with applicable laws, enter into a public-private partnership, cooperative agreement, memorandum of understanding, or similar agreement with a gateway community or a business in a gateway community.
